channel_news.title: IKEA and Best Buy Launch Shop-in-Shop Pilot Across 10 Stores in Florida and Texas
channel_news.summary: IKEA is partnering with Best Buy to place small shops within the latter's larger stores in a pilot project.

channel_news.title: Del Monte’s Bankruptcy Filing Reflects Cans, Costs and a Changing Grocery Aisle
channel_news.summary: After 139 years on U.S. grocery shelves, legacy canned-food brand Del Monte Foods has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection, citing...

channel_news.title: Why brands like Untuckit are experimenting with robot-made T-shirts
channel_news.summary: Some companies like Untuckit are experimenting with robotics to boost their "Made in USA" production.

channel_news.title: Guitar Center plots its turnaround, with a focus on serving ‘serious musicians’
channel_news.summary: Nearly six years after it declared bankruptcy, Guitar Center says its turnaround efforts are striking a chord. After redoing its merchandise, holding more events and refocusing on "serious musicians", Guitar Center is seeing sales climb, CEO Gabe Dalporto told Modern Retail.

channel_news.title: The World Cup provided a much-needed boost to the retail industry this summer
channel_news.summary: Despite some initial fears that fans wouldn't show up over hefty ticket prices, World Cup tourism helped lift sales in host cities. The tournament helped boost business for restaurants, retailers and even cannabis dispensaries.

channel_news.title: How Bloom Nutrition is expanding internationally
channel_news.summary: This year, Bloom Nutrition has been on a growth streak, and recently entered Australia, France and the U.K. Its Vice President of Global Growth, Joel Contartese, joins the Modern Retail Podcast to discuss how the brand has adapted its marketing, positioning and products for different markets.

channel_news.title: ‘The store is a lemonade stand’: Brands are lending their retail spaces to other brands for events
channel_news.summary: Brands like P.F. Candle Co. and Sorbara’s are lending their physical spaces to other brands for guest pop-ups. The strategy helps lift foot traffic and offset real estate costs.

channel_news.title: Sam’s Club takes Manhattan for wellness event with creators
channel_news.summary: On Thursday, Sam's Club hosted its first-ever Sam's Wellness Club, an exclusive event bringing together creators, media and merchants at a loft event space overlooking Midtown Manhattan. Here's why the club retailer is so keen on promoting its health and wellness offerings right now.

channel_news.title: Marketplace Briefing: What’s driving a surge in Amazon sales for 3 startups
channel_news.summary: Amid a challenging macroeconomic year, Amazon has proven to be a powerful sales engine for many digitally native startups. Modern Retail spoke with three startups — Suri, P.F. Candle Co, and Pretty Tasty Tea – about what they believe is driving their surge in Amazon sales this year.

channel_news.title: How toy manufacturer PlayMonster helped engineer a Hacky Sack revival
channel_news.summary: Encouraged by the resurgence of Hacky Sacks, toy company PlayMonster is developing about 10 new Hacky Sack styles. PlayMonster's CEO spoke with Modern Retail about keeping up with demand, and what the Hacky Sack revival says about the types of games and toys resonating with consumers right now.

channel_news.title: Salomon continues its US growth streak by launching at Foot Locker
channel_news.summary: Only two weeks after the cult French sneaker brand Salomon opened its largest store in New York City, it's making another big retail move: It's launching at Foot Locker.

channel_news.title: The state of agentic storefronts: How AI agents guide shoppers on frictionless, full-funnel journeys
channel_news.summary: This State of the Industry report, sponsored by Swap, explores how brands, retailers and agencies are adopting agentic commerce, including agentic storefronts, to enhance shopping experiences.
